lines.english: Lesson 47
lines.chinese: 第47课
lines.raw: Lesson 47 | 第47课

lines.english: Too high a price?
lines.chinese: 代价太高了吗？
lines.raw: Too high a price? | 代价太高了吗？

lines.english: What does the writer describe as an 'amusing old-fashioned source of noise' ?
lines.chinese: 作者把什么描述为“一个有趣的、老式的噪音来源”？
lines.raw: What does the writer describe as an 'amusing old-fashioned source of noise' ? | 作者把什么描述为“一个有趣的、老式的噪音来源”？

lines.english: Pollution is the price we pay for an overpopulated, overindustrialized planet.
lines.chinese: 污染是人口过剩、过度工业化的地球所付出的代价。
lines.raw: Pollution is the price we pay for an overpopulated, overindustrialized planet. | 污染是人口过剩、过度工业化的地球所付出的代价。

lines.english: When you come to think about it, there are only four ways you can deal with rubbish:
lines.chinese: 仔细想想，处理垃圾只有四种方法：
lines.raw: When you come to think about it, there are only four ways you can deal with rubbish: | 仔细想想，处理垃圾只有四种方法：

lines.english: dump it, burn it, turn it into something you can use again, attempt to produce less of it.
lines.chinese: 倾倒、焚烧、回收利用，或者试图减少垃圾产量。
lines.raw: dump it, burn it, turn it into something you can use again, attempt to produce less of it. | 倾倒、焚烧、回收利用，或者试图减少垃圾产量。

lines.english: We keep trying all four methods, but sheer volume of rubbish we produce worldwide threatens to overwhelm us.
lines.chinese: 我们一直在尝试这四种方法，但全球垃圾的巨大产量正威胁着要将我们淹没。
lines.raw: We keep trying all four methods, but sheer volume of rubbish we produce worldwide threatens to overwhelm us. | 我们一直在尝试这四种方法，但全球垃圾的巨大产量正威胁着要将我们淹没。

lines.english: Rubbish, however, is only part of the problem of polluting our planet.
lines.chinese: 然而，垃圾只是污染地球问题的一部分。
lines.raw: Rubbish, however, is only part of the problem of polluting our planet. | 然而，垃圾只是污染地球问题的一部分。

lines.english: The need to produce ever increasing quantities of cheap food leads to a different kind of pollution.
lines.chinese: 生产日益增长的廉价食物的需求导致了另一种污染。
lines.raw: The need to produce ever increasing quantities of cheap food leads to a different kind of pollution. | 生产日益增长的廉价食物的需求导致了另一种污染。

lines.english: Industriallized farming metheods produce cheap meat products: beef, pork and chicken.
lines.chinese: 工业化农业方法生产廉价肉类产品：牛肉、猪肉和鸡肉。
lines.raw: Industriallized farming metheods produce cheap meat products: beef, pork and chicken. | 工业化农业方法生产廉价肉类产品：牛肉、猪肉和鸡肉。

lines.english: The use of pesticides and fertilizers produces cheap grain and vegetables.
lines.chinese: 杀虫剂和化肥的使用生产廉价谷物和蔬菜。
lines.raw: The use of pesticides and fertilizers produces cheap grain and vegetables. | 杀虫剂和化肥的使用生产廉价谷物和蔬菜。

lines.english: The price we pay for cheap food may be already too high:
lines.chinese: 我们为廉价食物付出的代价可能已经太高了：
lines.raw: The price we pay for cheap food may be already too high: | 我们为廉价食物付出的代价可能已经太高了：

lines.english: Mad Cow Disease (BSE) in cattle, salmonella in chicken and eggs, and listeria in dairy products.
lines.chinese: 牛身上的疯牛病（BSE），鸡肉和鸡蛋中的沙门氏菌，以及奶制品中的李斯特菌。
lines.raw: Mad Cow Disease (BSE) in cattle, salmonella in chicken and eggs, and listeria in dairy products. | 牛身上的疯牛病（BSE），鸡肉和鸡蛋中的沙门氏菌，以及奶制品中的李斯特菌。

lines.english: And if you think you'll abandon meat and become a vegetarian,
lines.chinese: 如果你认为你会放弃肉类成为素食者，
lines.raw: And if you think you'll abandon meat and become a vegetarian, | 如果你认为你会放弃肉类成为素食者，

lines.english: you have the choice of very expensive organically-grown vegetables
lines.chinese: 你可以选择非常昂贵的有机蔬菜，
lines.raw: you have the choice of very expensive organically-grown vegetables | 你可以选择非常昂贵的有机蔬菜，

lines.english: or a steady diet of pesticides every time you think you're eating fresh salads and vegetables,
lines.chinese: 或者每次你以为在吃新鲜沙拉和蔬菜时，都伴随着大量的农药，
lines.raw: or a steady diet of pesticides every time you think you're eating fresh salads and vegetables, | 或者每次你以为在吃新鲜沙拉和蔬菜时，都伴随着大量的农药，

lines.english: or just having an innocent glass of water!
lines.chinese: 或者仅仅是喝一杯纯净的水！
lines.raw: or just having an innocent glass of water! | 或者仅仅是喝一杯纯净的水！

lines.english: However, there is an even more insidious kind of pollution that particularly affects urban area and invades our daily lives, and that is noise.
lines.chinese: 然而，还有一种更阴险的污染，它尤其影响城市地区并侵入我们的日常生活，那就是噪音。
lines.raw: However, there is an even more insidious kind of pollution that particularly affects urban area and invades our daily lives, and that is noise. | 然而，还有一种更阴险的污染，它尤其影响城市地区并侵入我们的日常生活，那就是噪音。

lines.english: Burglar alarms going off at any time of the day or night serve only to annoy passers-by and actually assist burglars to burgle.
lines.chinese: 昼夜不停响起的防盗警报只会惹恼路人，并实际上帮助窃贼入室盗窃。
lines.raw: Burglar alarms going off at any time of the day or night serve only to annoy passers-by and actually assist burglars to burgle. | 昼夜不停响起的防盗警报只会惹恼路人，并实际上帮助窃贼入室盗窃。

lines.english: Car alarms constantly scream at us in the street and are a source of profound irritation.
lines.chinese: 汽车警报在街上不断对我们尖叫，是极度恼人的来源。
lines.raw: Car alarms constantly scream at us in the street and are a source of profound irritation. | 汽车警报在街上不断对我们尖叫，是极度恼人的来源。

lines.english: A recent survey of the effects of noise revealed (surprisingly?)
lines.chinese: 最近一项关于噪音影响的调查显示（令人惊讶的是？）
lines.raw: A recent survey of the effects of noise revealed (surprisingly?) | 最近一项关于噪音影响的调查显示（令人惊讶的是？）

lines.english: that dogs barking incessantly in the night rated the highest form of noise pollution on a scale ranging from 1 to 7.
lines.chinese: 狗在夜间不停吠叫被评为1到7级噪音污染中最高的形式。
lines.raw: that dogs barking incessantly in the night rated the highest form of noise pollution on a scale ranging from 1 to 7. | 狗在夜间不停吠叫被评为1到7级噪音污染中最高的形式。

lines.english: The survey revealed a large number of sources of noise that we really dislike.
lines.chinese: 该调查揭示了许多我们真正不喜欢的噪音来源。
lines.raw: The survey revealed a large number of sources of noise that we really dislike. | 该调查揭示了许多我们真正不喜欢的噪音来源。

lines.english: Lawn mowers whining on a summer's day, late-night parties in apartment blocks,
lines.chinese: 夏日里割草机的嗡嗡声，公寓楼里的深夜派对，
lines.raw: Lawn mowers whining on a summer's day, late-night parties in apartment blocks, | 夏日里割草机的嗡嗡声，公寓楼里的深夜派对，

lines.english: noisy neighbours, vehicles of all kinds,
lines.chinese: 吵闹的邻居，各种车辆，
lines.raw: noisy neighbours, vehicles of all kinds, | 吵闹的邻居，各种车辆，

lines.english: especially large container trucks thundering through quiet villages,
lines.chinese: 特别是大型集装箱卡车轰隆隆地穿过宁静的村庄，
lines.raw: especially large container trucks thundering through quiet villages, | 特别是大型集装箱卡车轰隆隆地穿过宁静的村庄，

lines.english: planes and helicopters flying overhead, large radios carried round in public places and played at maximum volume.
lines.chinese: 飞机和直升机从头顶飞过，公共场所里大声播放的收音机。
lines.raw: planes and helicopters flying overhead, large radios carried round in public places and played at maximum volume. | 飞机和直升机从头顶飞过，公共场所里大声播放的收音机。

lines.english: New technology has also made its own contribution to noise.
lines.chinese: 新技术也对噪音有所“贡献”。
lines.raw: New technology has also made its own contribution to noise. | 新技术也对噪音有所“贡献”。

lines.english: A lot of people object to mobile phones, especially when they are used in public places like restaurant or on public transport.
lines.chinese: 很多人反对手机，尤其是在餐馆或公共交通工具等公共场所使用手机。
lines.raw: A lot of people object to mobile phones, especially when they are used in public places like restaurant or on public transport. | 很多人反对手机，尤其是在餐馆或公共交通工具等公共场所使用手机。

lines.english: Loud conversations on mobile phones invade our thoughts or interrupt the pleasure of meeting friends for a quiet chat.
lines.chinese: 手机上的大声对话侵扰我们的思绪，或者打断我们与朋友安静聊天的乐趣。
lines.raw: Loud conversations on mobile phones invade our thoughts or interrupt the pleasure of meeting friends for a quiet chat. | 手机上的大声对话侵扰我们的思绪，或者打断我们与朋友安静聊天的乐趣。

lines.english: The noise pollution survey revealed a rather surprising and possibly amusing old-fashioned source of noise.
lines.chinese: 噪音污染调查揭示了一个相当令人惊讶且可能有趣的、老式的噪音来源。
lines.raw: The noise pollution survey revealed a rather surprising and possibly amusing old-fashioned source of noise. | 噪音污染调查揭示了一个相当令人惊讶且可能有趣的、老式的噪音来源。

lines.english: It turned out to be snoring! Men were found to be the worst offenders.
lines.chinese: 结果是打鼾！男性被发现是最大的“罪魁祸首”。
lines.raw: It turned out to be snoring! Men were found to be the worst offenders. | 结果是打鼾！男性被发现是最大的“罪魁祸首”。

lines.english: It was revealed that 20% of men in their mid-thirties snore.
lines.chinese: 调查显示，20%的三十多岁男性会打鼾。
lines.raw: It was revealed that 20% of men in their mid-thirties snore. | 调查显示，20%的三十多岁男性会打鼾。

lines.english: This figure rises to a staggering 60% of men in their sixties.
lines.chinese: 这个数字在六十多岁男性中飙升至惊人的60%。
lines.raw: This figure rises to a staggering 60% of men in their sixties. | 这个数字在六十多岁男性中飙升至惊人的60%。

lines.english: Against these figures, it was found that only 5% of women snore regularly,
lines.chinese: 与这些数字相比，只有5%的女性会规律性打鼾，
lines.raw: Against these figures, it was found that only 5% of women snore regularly, | 与这些数字相比，只有5%的女性会规律性打鼾，

lines.english: while the rest are constantly woken or kept awake by their trumpeting partners.
lines.chinese: 而其余的女性则不断地被她们打鼾的伴侣吵醒或保持清醒。
lines.raw: while the rest are constantly woken or kept awake by their trumpeting partners. | 而其余的女性则不断地被她们打鼾的伴侣吵醒或保持清醒。

lines.english: Whatever the source of noise, one thing is certain: silence, it seems, has become a golden memory.
lines.chinese: 无论噪音来源是什么，有一件事是肯定的：宁静，似乎已成为一段美好的回忆。
lines.raw: Whatever the source of noise, one thing is certain: silence, it seems, has become a golden memory. | 无论噪音来源是什么，有一件事是肯定的：宁静，似乎已成为一段美好的回忆。
